content:
# Barista Skill Level Assessment

**Tool ID:** T042-02
**Block:** B042 (Espresso Excellence Standards)
**Domain:** Coffee & Product Quality (D03_coffee_product)
**Level:** L2
**Format:** Checklist
**Role:** Head Barista
**Time Required:** 15-25 minutes per person
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

This is a system-building tool. It takes your daily practices and turns them into something structured, repeatable, and visible to your team.

Not another tick-box exercise. This assessment maps where each barista stands across three skill levels, so you know exactly who needs what kind of support and who is ready to teach others.

**Built for Head Barista working in Coffee & Product Quality.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

Practical assessment checklists for three skill levels (Competent, Proficient, Advanced) covering espresso preparation, sensory evaluation, troubleshooting, and training capability. Use to map team skills, plan development, and track progression.

This tool supports **Espresso Excellence Standards** (B042) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Head Barista (assessing), any barista (being assessed)
**Time Required:** 15-25 minutes (observe across 3-5 drinks during service)
**Frequency:** New baristas: end of week 2. All baristas: quarterly. Before any promotion or pay review.

**What You Will Need:**
- This assessment form (printed)
- Scales (0.1g accuracy)
- Shot timer (or phone stopwatch)
- Probe thermometer
- Cupping spoons (for sensory section)
- Knock box, cleaning supplies at the station

**Steps:**
1. Assess during a real shift -- not a rehearsal
2. Observe 3-5 drinks being made without intervening
3. Score each skill: PASS (consistently demonstrated) or NOT YET (inconsistent or absent)
4. Determine overall level: highest level where ALL items pass
5. Discuss results, agree development areas, set a reassessment date
6. Update T019-01 training tracker

---

## Checklist

### Assessment Information

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| Barista assessed | |
| Current role | |
| Date | |
| Assessed by | |
| Current skill level (before assessment) | Competent / Proficient / Advanced / New |

### Level 1: Competent Barista

*Minimum standard for working the espresso station independently. ALL items must pass to achieve this level.*

| # | Skill | Pass | Not Yet | Notes |
|---|-------|------|---------|-------|
| 1.1 | Doses correct weight consistently (18g +/- 0.3g for double) | P | NY | Weighed: _____g |
| 1.2 | Distributes and tamps evenly (level tamp, consistent pressure) | P | NY | |
| 1.3 | Pulls espresso within recipe parameters (36g +/- 2g in 25-30s) | P | NY | Yield: _____g Time: _____s |
| 1.4 | Purges group head before inserting portafilter | P | NY | |
| 1.5 | Steams milk to correct temperature (62-65 deg C, not above 70 deg C) | P | NY | Measured: _____ deg C |
| 1.6 | Creates microfoam with no visible large bubbles | P | NY | |
| 1.7 | Pours basic latte art (heart or tulip, recognisable shape) | P | NY | |
| 1.8 | Knocks puck cleanly and wipes portafilter basket | P | NY | |
| 1.9 | Cleans steam wand immediately after each use (purge + wipe) | P | NY | |
| 1.10 | Knows the current coffee origin and can tell a customer the basics | P | NY | |

**Level 1 result:** _____ / 10 Pass. Level achieved: Yes / No

### Level 2: Proficient Barista

*All Level 1 items must pass PLUS all items below. This barista can work peak service confidently and begin training new starters.*

| # | Skill | Pass | Not Yet | Notes |
|---|-------|------|---------|-------|
| 2.1 | Adjusts grind to maintain recipe when shot runs fast or slow | P | NY | |
| 2.2 | Can dial in a new coffee bag within 3 test shots | P | NY | |
| 2.3 | Identifies common taste faults: sour (under-extracted) vs bitter (over-extracted) | P | NY | |
| 2.4 | Manages 4+ drink queue without quality compromise during peak | P | NY | |
| 2.5 | Steams milk for different textures (flat white vs cappuccino foam level) | P | NY | |
| 2.6 | Pours consistent latte art on 9 out of 10 drinks | P | NY | |
| 2.7 | Can make all menu espresso drinks from memory (recipe and technique) | P | NY | |
| 2.8 | Performs daily backflush correctly (T048-01 procedure) | P | NY | |
| 2.9 | Can explain dose, yield, and time relationship to a new barista | P | NY | |
| 2.10 | Manages alternative milks correctly (oat: 60-65 deg C, soy: below 60 deg C) | P | NY | |

**Level 2 result:** _____ / 10 Pass. Level achieved: Yes / No

### Level 3: Advanced Barista

*All Level 1 and 2 items must pass PLUS all items below. This barista can lead the espresso programme and train the team.*

| # | Skill | Pass | Not Yet | Notes |
|---|-------|------|---------|-------|
| 3.1 | Evaluates espresso using SCA flavour wheel vocabulary (3+ descriptors) | P | NY | |
| 3.2 | Can adjust recipe for a new single-origin coffee based on tasting notes | P | NY | |
| 3.3 | Identifies when grinder burrs need replacement (taste and shot time changes) | P | NY | |
| 3.4 | Conducts comparative cupping (side-by-side evaluation of 2+ coffees) | P | NY | |
| 3.5 | Trains a new barista from zero to Level 1 competence within 2 weeks | P | NY | |
| 3.6 | Manages water quality basics (knows current TDS, understands its impact) | P | NY | TDS reading: _____ ppm |
| 3.7 | Can troubleshoot machine issues (pressure, temperature, group head problems) | P | NY | |
| 3.8 | Communicates with roaster about coffee feedback (uses professional vocabulary) | P | NY | |

**Level 3 result:** _____ / 8 Pass. Level achieved: Yes / No

### Overall Assessment Summary

| Level | Required Passes | Achieved | Result |
|-------|----------------|----------|--------|
| Level 1: Competent | 10/10 | | Yes / No |
| Level 2: Proficient | 10/10 (plus L1) | | Yes / No |
| Level 3: Advanced | 8/8 (plus L1+L2) | | Yes / No |

**Overall skill level:** _____

**Strengths:** _____

**Development areas:** _____

**Agreed next steps:**
1. _____
2. _____
3. _____

**Reassessment date:** _____

### Sign-Off

| Role | Name | Signature | Date |
|------|------|-----------|------|
| Assessor (Head Barista) | | | |
| Barista assessed | | | |

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Assessment Only During Quiet Service

**Why it happens:** Head Barista assesses during a calm period. Every drink is perfect. But the real test is during a 15-drink queue at 08:30.
**How to prevent:** Assess Level 2 and 3 skills specifically during peak service (07:30-10:00). Level 1 can be assessed anytime.
**If it is already happening:** Add a note to Level 2 item 2.4: observe during the next morning rush specifically.

### Risk 2: Latte Art Prioritised Over Espresso Quality

**Why it happens:** Beautiful rosetta on top of a sour, under-extracted shot. Looks great, tastes terrible. Latte art is visible, extraction quality is not.
**How to prevent:** Always assess items 1.1-1.3 (dose, distribution, extraction) BEFORE assessing latte art. A perfect pour on a bad shot is still a fail.
**If it is already happening:** Taste every assessed shot before looking at the art. Score espresso quality first.

### Risk 3: No Development Path for Staff Who Want to Progress

**Why it happens:** Assessment is done, scores are recorded, but nothing changes. Competent baristas stay Competent forever. Frustration builds.
**How to prevent:** Pair development areas with specific actions: "To pass 2.2, practice dialling in with me every Monday morning for 10 minutes before open." Set a reassessment date.
**If it is already happening:** Choose your most motivated Competent barista. Create a 4-week plan to get them to Proficient. Show the team that progression is real.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** Add advanced brewing methods (pour-over, AeroPress, batch brew) to Level 3. Include filter coffee competencies.
**Chain / Franchise:** Align levels with your brand barista progression programme. Use this as the practical assessment component.
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Focus on Level 1 only. Speed and consistency matter more than advanced sensory skills in high-volume environments.
**Bakery Cafe:** Add items for pairing coffee with baked goods (can recommend a coffee to match a pastry).

**Making it yours:**
- Display the skill levels (anonymised) in the staff area as a team progression board
- Adjust espresso recipe parameters to match your current coffee and machine setup
- Add alternative milk brands by name if they require specific techniques
- Celebrate level-ups publicly -- recognition drives motivation

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T042-01 -- Cupping Session Guide (L1, template)
- T042-03 -- Monthly Espresso Quality Review Template (L2, dashboard)
- T042-04 -- Roaster Communication Log (L2, tracker)
- T042-05 -- Espresso Programme Annual Review (L3, template)

**Related blocks:**
- B039
- B040
- B041

**Parent block:** B042 -- Espresso Excellence Standards
**Module:** M03: Coffee Craft & Menu Excellence
---

*Tool Version 1.0 -- OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
